assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been declared and recognised as an intercompany monetary receivable/payable.

The entities involved have different functional currencies, so the foreign exchange differences on that intragroup monetary item are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.
Any designated hedge relationship would satisfy the formal designation, documentation and hedge effectiveness requirements in IFRS 9 paragraph 6.4.1.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised foreign-currency intragroup receivable can be considered under the fair value hedge model if its exchange differences remain exposed in consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The same foreign-currency exposure on a recognised receivable can also be analysed under the cash flow hedge model because exchange rate changes create variability in functional-currency cash flows affecting profit or loss.
